title: Benzoin megaphyllum
intro: Benzoin megaphyllum, also known as Sumatran benzoin, is a species of plant in the family Styracaceae, native to Sumatra in Indonesia. The resinous sap of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Benzoin megaphyllum include benzoin resin, styrax, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Benzoin megaphyllum include topical creams, ointments, and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Benzoin megaphyllum is a species of plant in the family Styracaceae. It is native to Borneo and is a type of evergreen tree. Benzoin megaphyllum is closely related to other species within the same family, including the fragrant tree Styrax. Further classification of Benzoin megaphyllum is at the genus level, where it belongs to the genus Styrax. However, it is often considered to be a synonym for Styrax megaphylla.

These include benzoin resin, styrax, and other terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal and aromatic properties. The resin is also rich in vanillin, a compound that gives the plant its distinct aroma and flavor.
